plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs to eliminate high risk.
Trim QQQI and SPY to reduce concentration and risk.
Add NVDA and DDOG for their strong scanner readings.
This plan builds a cash buffer and focuses on quality.
It aims for capital preservation with lower volatility.
Expect a lower target return due to reduced risk.
conservative.read: This plan prioritizes capital preservation, aiming for a modest ~1.5% monthly return. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk, leading to lower potential drawdowns but also capping upside potential.

plan: Trim SOXL and exit SPAL to manage leverage.
Reduce SPY and QQQI to decrease concentration.
Add NVDA and DDOG for their strong risk/reward.
This plan balances growth with risk management.
It aims for moderate growth with controlled drawdowns.
Expect a target return around 2.5% monthly.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets ~2.5% monthly return by managing risk first. It reduces leverage and concentration while adding high-conviction names, aiming for a mix of growth and stability with controlled drawdowns.

plan: Increase SOXL exposure and add a small SPAL position.
Trim SPY and exit QQQI to reallocate capital.
Add NVDU and DDOG for their high scanner scores.
Increase CRWD as a strong performer.
This plan concentrates risk in high-conviction names.
It aims for aggressive growth but with higher drawdowns.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan aims for ~4% monthly return by concentrating capital in high-scoring setups and increasing leveraged ETFs. Expect higher potential gains but also significantly larger drawdowns and amplified risk due to leverage and concentration. Daily rebalancing of leveraged ETFs can impact returns.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs and high-beta single names.
Significantly increase QQQI for income generation.
Trim SPY and QQQ to reduce overall risk.
Add a small NVDA position for potential upside.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions.
It aims for low drawdowns and reliable income.
income.read: This income-focused plan targets ~1% monthly return by prioritizing yield and capital preservation. It cuts leverage and high-beta names, focusing on income-generating ETFs like QQQI. While distributions are prioritized, NAV can still fall in market downturns.
